channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: We Reinvented PHP but 10x Slower: Why React Server Components Might Be an Architectural Dead End
item.description: We Reinvented PHP but 10x Slower: Why React Server Components Might Be an Architectural Dead End In 2015, deploying a blog took 30 seconds. FTP upload, done. In 2026, I spent 4 hours deploying a...

item.title: Fast Delivery Isn't Enough. You Need Fast Feedback.
item.description: It's demo day. Claude Code knocked out the data layer migration in two days. So the team pulled in more backend work—cleaner API contracts, better abstractions. Might as well use the time. The...

item.title: I Built an Android App Without Writing a Single Line of Code (Using AI Agents)
item.description: I Built an Android App Without Writing a Single Line of Code AI tools are changing how we build software. Recently, I experimented with a different development approach: building an application...

item.title: I audited a codebase written by Devin 3.0. It was a nightmare.
item.description: We aren't just shipping features faster; we are shipping technical debt faster. If you treat AI as an architect instead of an intern, you are building a legacy graveyard that will be impossible to...

item.title: AI tools can unmask anonymous accounts
item.description: Do you have a Reddit alt, secret X, finsta, or Glassdoor account you trash your boss with? AI might have just made it a lot easier to unmask you. That's the conclusion of a recently published...

item.title: How to Never Quit Your Coding Journey
item.description: So a lot of times, we look at the tech industry and the advice for people looking to get in is all technical. It's always talking about learning this framework, then learning that one, then...

item.title: Pockr | Part 6 — Test Results and What's Next
item.description: Test Results, Firebase Test Lab, and What's Next for Pockr Part 6 of 6 — the final chapter of building Pockr, a single APK that runs Docker on non-rooted Android. ← Part 5: Debugging a VM Restart...

item.title: How to set up Secure OpenClaw and power it with 850+ SaaS Apps 🦞🔒
item.description: OpenClaw has been showing up in my feed way too much, so I finally sat down and tested it properly, and yeah, it comes with a few real problems. In this post, I’ll cover what OpenClaw is, how to...

item.title: Pockr | Part 5 — Debugging the VM Restart Loop

item.title: How 1,000+ customer calls shaped a breakout enterprise AI startup
item.description: On this episode of Build Mode, David Park joins Isabelle Johannessen to discuss how he and his team are intentionally iterating, fundraising, and scaling Narada.

item.title: Lio raises $30M from Andreessen Horowitz and others to automate enterprise procurement
item.description: AI procurement startup Lio announced a $30 million Series A in a round led by Andreessen Horowitz.

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of...

item.title: Anthropic makes last-ditch effort to salvage deal with Pentagon after blowup
item.description: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is reportedly back at the negotiating table with the Department of Defense in an attempt to salvage the company's relationship with the US military and prevent it from...

item.title: Hacked App Part of US/Israeli Propaganda Campaign Against Iran
item.description: Wired has the story: Shortly after the first set of explosions, Iranians received bursts of notifications on their phones. They came not from the government advising caution, but from an...

item.title: Nothing’s Headphone A are something worth considering
item.description: After the release of the Headphone 1 last year - Nothing's first over-ear headphones - the company is back with the Headphone A. They're a slimmed-down over-ear option that keep the Nothing design...

item.title: Nothing is finally covering up with the slim, metal Phone 4A Pro
item.description: Today Nothing has revealed the 4A and 4A Pro, its latest midrange phones. The two look startlingly different from one another, with varying designs and Glyph Light interfaces. The Pro is...

item.title: How much wildfire prevention is too much?
item.description: The race to prevent the worst wildfires has been an increasingly high-tech one. Companies are proposing AI fire detection systems and drones that can stamp out early blazes. And now, one Canadian...

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong....

item.title: Bill Gates’ nuclear company is the first to get approval to build next-gen reactor
item.description: Bill Gates-founded nuclear energy startup TerraPower has been granted approval from the Nuclear Regulatory Commission to build a next-generation reactor in Wyoming. This is the first...

item.title: APT28-Linked Campaign Deploys BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Russian cyber campaign that has targeted Ukrainian entities with two previously undocumented malware families named BadPaw and MeowMeow....

item.title: Online harassment is entering its AI era
item.description: Scott Shambaugh didn’t think twice when he denied an AI agent’s request to contribute to matplotlib, a software library that he helps manage. Like many open-source projects, matplotlib has been...

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Takes Down Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Linked to 64,000 Attacks
item.description: Tycoon 2FA, one of the prominent phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) toolkits that allowed cybercriminals to stage adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) credential harvesting attacks at scale, was dismantled...

item.title: FBI and Europol Seize LeakBase Forum Used to Trade Stolen Credentials
item.description: A joint law enforcement operation has dismantled LeakBase, one of the world's largest online forums for cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and cybercrime tools. The LeakBase forum, per the...

item.title: Did Live Nation punish a venue by taking Billie Eilish away?
item.description: John Abbamondi had orders to let the CEO of Ticketmaster down easy. In April 2021, Abbamondi was the CEO of BSE Global, the company that ran Brooklyn arena the Barclays Center. BSE Global's...

item.title: A new video from the White House mixes Call of Duty footage with actual video of Iran strikes
item.description: On Wednesday, the White House posted a video of actual military strikes on Iran in the style usually seen in Call of Duty highlight videos, and started the video with a clip from Call of Duty. The...

item.title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic, but his explanation raises more questions than it answers
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday that his company's investments in OpenAI and Anthropic will likely be its last — but his explanation may not tell the whole story.

item.title: Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers
item.description: Leaders from Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, Amazon, and xAI met with President Donald Trump today to sign a "rate payer protection pledge." It's one way they're responding to growing...

item.title: Tim Sweeney signed away his right to criticize Google until 2032
item.description: Epic CEO Tim Sweeney might be one of the most outspoken people in the history of the world. He fought two of the world's most valuable and powerful companies almost all the way to the US Supreme...
